description.en: This is the object passed to the constructor’s template argument. In general, you shouldn’t change it, but read-only access is not enforced.

excerpt.es: This is the object passed to the constructor’s template argument. In general, you shouldn’t change it, but read-only access is not enforced.
excerpt.en: This is the object passed to the constructor’s template argument. In general, you shouldn’t change it, but read-only access is not enforced.

description.en: The constructor takes a single argument which is the template string. substitute(mapping={}, /, **kwds)¶ Performs the template substitution, returning a new string. mapping is any dictionary-like object with keys that match the placeholders in the template. Alternatively, you can provide keyword arguments, where the keywords are the placeholders. When both mapping and kwds are given and there are duplicates, the placeholders from kwds take precedence. safe_substitute(mapping={}, /, **kwds)¶ L...
